channel.description: Recent content in home on Notes on Technology

item.title: why you should use RSS
item.description: Why you should use RSS RSS is the path to freedom Lorem Curabitur laoreet, turpis id placerat lobortis, sapien tortor venenatis leo, sit amet convallis tortor augue eget ex. Mae1cenas metus velit, egestas sed nisl vitae, dapibus venenatis urna. Phasellus mattis dignissim enim, ut tempor mauris mollis eu. Curabitur ac neque consequat, vehicula enim ut, vestibulum mauris. Sed rhoncus odio at massa tristique, id lobortis tortor scelerisque. In facilisis, ligula non posuere eleifend, dui ipsum egestas elit, eu pretium ligula massa eu erat.

item.title: This is a example page
item.description: This is a footnote 1 and here is a link zero to monero, this is awesome! A cool program planet another link Caterpillars become butterflies ↩︎

item.title: Desktop Setup
item.description: In this post I will detail How I have setup my compuer. Similar to usesthis. Things I want I want a keyboard centric experience I want to be able to do the most possible things 1 software I use music player: foobar2k book manager: zotero browser: chrome and firefox text-editor: emacs through WSL file explorer: other: chocolatey why use Emacs Emacs (run through wsl) is by far the most capabable keyboard centric editor.

item.title: Tracking Music
Colophon
item.description: Fancy word people use for the design and building of a website. influences https://dpitt.me/ https://grahamc.com/ https://p1k3.com/ https://blog.jemu.name/ checklist I made a checklist of things that I think are virtues in a website. See it here. Not everything is implement (most isn’t) but we have a roadmap. stack sample test awesome 1 cool ↩︎

item.title: Do you need Spotify? (good YouTube channels)
item.description: Ambiance has a lot of great electronic albums (mostly 1990s). Saturn Archives post a lot of avant garde pieces that are interesting. Jimmy Eden Moon mainly 90s hiphop. Kuma’s campfire Good catalog of Japanese albums a lot pretty avant garde. Xerf Xpec Wonderful collection of Japanese Jazz Fusion. Terminal Passage Mainly psychedelic and Prog but very ecletic. Peter Niktos Mainly 60s and 70s psydelic. Elshay Yehezkel Awesome Italian music. Nicolás Guzmán Ecletic and extremly wonderful.

item.title: Review of Books on Finance
item.description: STILL A WORK IN PROGRESS A survey of results on the topic, with a emphasis on the most practical. Note of course, these are just the ones I’ve read, there are obviously other amazing finance books but I’ve not read them. the list is: 4 Pillars of Investing (Bernstein 2002) Ivy Portfolio (Faber 2009) Global Asset Allocation (Faber 2015) Simple Path to Wealth (Collin 2016) Simple Path to Wealth (Collin 2016) Index Funds: The 12-step Program for Active Investors (Hebner 2007) Guru Investor (Forehand, Reese 2009) The Misbehavior of Markets (Mandelbrot 2004) Millionaire Next Door (Stanley 1996) Early Retirement Extreme (Fisker 2010) Tightwad Gazette (Dacyczyn 1992) In regard to what is most important in understand and getting some benefits from finance I would say Mandelbrot if you want to understand the general field of finance and Faber if you want a good portfolio.
